Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment with metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals protein, glucose, ketones, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance, raising concern for nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for underlying etiology.
